channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel lets Palestinians return to northern Gaza for the first time in over a year as truce holds
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel on Monday began allowing thousands of Palestinians to return to the heavily destroyed north of the Gaza Strip for the first time since the early weeks of the 15-month war with Hamas, in accordance with a fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arrest quotas for ICE officers 'a bad idea': William Enyart
item.description: Enacting a daily arrest quota for ICE agents would be "asking for an abuse of their police powers," Ret. U.S. Army Major Gen. William Enyart said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, Fox 5

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — After a half-century of public silence, a freelance photographer from Vietnam has asserted he took one of the most renowned and impactful photos of the 20th century — the image of a naked girl fleeing a napalm attack in South Vietnam that has long been credited to a staff phot
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fewer women are getting tested for cancer: Survey
item.description: The Hologic Women's Health Index's 2023 data found a decrease in testing for any cancer among women — a first in the survey's four-year history.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Ryan Pressly waives no-trade clause to join the Chicago Cubs, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are finalizing a trade for Ryan Pressly, adding the right-hander to the back end of their bullpen in their second major offseason deal with the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: Court records
item.description: Matthew and Sarah McGrath filed a lawsuit in February 2023, claiming a house built for them a year earlier had numerous defects,
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: ICE detains nearly 50 people in 'makeshift nightclub' raid in Denver area: DEA
item.description: "For the last several months, DEA has been running an ongoing investigation against the Venezuelan gang called TdA," special agent Jonathan Pullen said.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: ICE begins 'enhanced targeted operations' in Austin
item.description: A series of ICE raids were conducted in Austin on Sunday, according to sources from the Drug Enforcement Administration.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penske's pair of Porsches dominate as The Captain wins Rolex 24 at Daytona for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Roger Penske started his 59th season in motorsports the same way he closed last year — as a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan cadet disarms 5-year-old with gun while serving subpoena
item.description: The cadet asked the child to have an adult come to the door. When the child returned they were armed with a loaded handgun.
item.dc:creator: Corinne Moore, NewsNation

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, KRON4

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida man arrested for allegedly calling for Trump assassination on Facebook; Secret Service investigating
item.description: A Florida man was arrested after allegedly calling for the assassination of President Donald Trump on Facebook. The Secret Service is investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rain in Southern California will aid firefighters but create a risk of toxic ash runoff
item.description: Rain that began falling in parched Southern California will aid firefighters mopping up multiple wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation's defense secretary late Friday in a dramatic tie-breaking vote, swatting back questions about his qualifications to lead the Pentagon amid allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders agree to hire 73-year-old Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: Pete Carroll has a youthful energy that many people half his age don't possess, and the Raiders hope that enthusiasm — and, more importantly, his proven track record — translates to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma wants federal inmate transferred so he can be put to death
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma's top prosecutor asked the federal Bureau of Prisons to transfer an inmate to state custody so that he could be executed for his role in the kidnapping and killing of a 77-year-old woman in 1999.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spirit Airlines passengers can now be removed from flight for 'offensive' tattoos, clothing
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines could now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or piece of clothing is deemed offensive, according to the airline's updated policy.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Activists to 'resist' Trump through know-your-rights campaign
item.description: The pulse of El Paso’s immigrant community can be felt through the calls streaming into a small white “BNHR” building on Piedras Street over the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, ABC 4

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Judge to weigh genetic evidence and search warrants in University of Idaho quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Attorneys for a man charged with murder in connection with the killings of four University of Idaho students are asking a judge to throw out most of the evidence in the case because they say it all hinges on an unconstitutional genetic investigation process.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4
